description: French liaison is a sandhi process where a word-final silent consonant becomes pronounced when the next word begins with a vowel. This creates an allophonic alternation between zero and a consonant allophone (e.g., [z] in 'vous avez' → [vu.z‿a.ve]).
sources: Morin, Y. (1986). _Liaison and the representation of morphological information_. In _Papers from the 22nd Regional Meeting of the Chicago Linguistic Society_ (pp. 307-322).
Kilarski, K. (2009). _A critical review of the notion of obligatory liaison in French_. In _Proceedings of the Annual Boston University Conference on Language Development_ (Vol. 33, pp. 1-12).
Gess, R., & Lyche, C. (2004). _French liaison_. In _Journal of French Language Studies_ (Vol. 14, No. 2, pp. 211-233).
